name: Pico Yordas Loop Trail
name_original: Pico Yordas o Burín desde Liegos – Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre
description: Hike the difficult 11.3-mile Pico Yordas or Burín trail from Liegos for panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir and Cantabrian Mountains.
region_intro: Hiking around Acebedo offers diverse landscapes within the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. The region features the Cantabrian Mountains and panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir. Hikers can explore a variety of natural features, including impressive mountain peaks, dramatic passes, and picturesque waterfalls. The terrain ranges from accessible paths to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Acebedo The most popular hiking route is Pico Yordas Loop Trail, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views of the Riaño reservoir and the Cantabrian Mountains. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Saltadero from Acebedo – circular Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This circular hike is accessible and suitable for various fitness levels, taking about 1 hour 15 minutes. Local hikers also love the Peña Ten Circular Trail, an 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail leading through mountain landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Acebedo is defined by the Cantabrian Mountains, the Riaño reservoir, and diverse natural monuments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging treks. The routes in Acebedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Acebedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Acebedo?
A: Acebedo offers a selection of 11 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents within the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking trails in Acebedo?
A: Yes, for an easy and family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050121631/el-saltadero-from-acebedo-circular-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltadero from Acebedo – circular Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre&lt;/a&gt;. This circular hike is 4.2 km long with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for most fitness levels and families.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Acebedo?
A: Hiking around Acebedo offers breathtaking panoramic views, especially of the Riaño reservoir and the majestic Cantabrian Mountains. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050099424/pico-yordas-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Yordas Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide expansive vistas of these prominent natural features. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcayo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Acebedo area?
A: Yes, Acebedo features several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050141394/pena-ten-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ten Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through stunning mountain landscapes. Another accessible circular hike is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050121631/el-saltadero-from-acebedo-circular-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltadero from Acebedo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I explore near Acebedo's hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, the region boasts impressive natural attractions. You can discover picturesque waterfalls and deep canyons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirva Valley&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz Oscura&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features various mountain peaks and dramatic passes, all within the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Acebedo?
A: The diverse landscapes of Acebedo, from accessible paths to challenging mountain ascents, offer hiking opportunities across multiple seasons. While specific seasonal conditions vary, spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. For higher altitude trails, summer offers more stable weather, though some routes may require alpine experience.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Acebedo or the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre?
A: The Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, where Acebedo is located, generally welcomes dogs on trails. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific rules may apply in certain protected areas or during breeding seasons, so check local signage.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes in Acebedo?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050099424/pico-yordas-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Yordas Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.2 km route with significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38186119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ten loop from La Uña&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 16.2 km with over 900 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Acebedo?
A: The hiking routes in Acebedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning views of the Riaño reservoir and the Cantabrian Mountains, as well as the diverse terrain that offers options for all ability levels.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Acebedo?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead can vary, many popular starting points for hikes in Acebedo, such as those leading to Pico Yordas or El Saltadero, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes around Acebedo?
A: Yes, for a moderately challenging hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14990211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yordas Peak (1,967 m) loop from Parking Valle de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;. This 8.6 km route involves a significant ascent but is less demanding than the full Pico Yordas Loop, offering a good balance for those seeking a rewarding experience without extreme difficulty.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hikes in Acebedo?
A: The region around Acebedo is known for its natural beauty, including picturesque waterfalls and deep canyons. While specific named waterfalls on individual trails are not detailed, the general landscape of the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre suggests opportunities to encounter these natural features, especially after rainfall.
tips:
Pico Yordas or Buríon is located 1,967 meters above sea level and is one of the most emblematic peaks of the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park.
The ascent is perfectly signposted with milestones and does not offer major complications.
From the summit we can see all the mountains around Riaño, highlighting Peñas Pintas and Pico Gilbo. We also enjoy spectacular views of the Alto de la Rasa, the Palencia Mountain, the Picos de Europa and the Mampodre Massif, without forgetting the main peaks of the Cantabrian Mountains.
Located at 1,689 meters above sea level, Collado Burín offers us a fantastic panoramic view of the mountains that surround us, like the Burín peak itself.

Wide dirt track that is located at a crossing point of the PR-32 "Liegos-Acebedo".
We will see a mural that refers to the economic engine of the Riaño Valley: horse and cattle farming.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike Pico Yordas or Burín from Liegos?
faqs.answer: The full hike, covering over 18 km with more than 900 meters of elevation gain, usually takes around 6 to 7 hours to complete, including stops for rest and enjoying the views.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult and is not recommended for beginners. It requires a good level of physical preparation due to its length, significant elevation gain, and challenging sections, especially the final ascent to the summit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the way to Pico Yordas or Burín?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. It starts with a comfortable, wide track through meadows, then transitions into a narrower, sometimes steep, and winding path through the beautiful Tendeña beech forest. After the forest, it opens up to more exposed areas. The final kilometer to the summit is a demanding diagonal section over rocky, stony ground with a significant incline.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route to the summit?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery, from green meadows and the dense Tendeña beech forest to panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir and surrounding mountains. Key points of interest include the Yordas (1,967 m) summit itself, the Burín Pass, and the Horse and Cattle Mural on PR-32, Riaño Valley. The summit offers exceptional views of the Cantabrian Mountains, including the Mampodre massif and distant Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally from late spring through early autumn. During these months, the weather is typically more stable, and the trails are clear of snow, offering the most pleasant conditions for the challenging ascent.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead in Liegos?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Liegos. You can usually find parking within the village, near the beginning of the Camino de San Pelayo, which is the initial wide track for the route.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Yordas or Burín trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and livestock along the route. The challenging terrain and length mean your dog should also be fit for a difficult hike.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for hiking in the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail within the Parque Regional de la Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. However, always respect local regulations regarding nature conservation and wildlife.

faqs.question: Is there fresh water available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes by the Fuente la Tejal, which can be a welcome source of fresh water. However, it's always advisable to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of this demanding hike.

faqs.question: Why is the peak known by two names, Yordas and Burín?
faqs.answer: The peak is famously known by two names due to local tradition. Residents of Riaño refer to it as Pico Yordas, while those from Liegos call it Pico Burín (or Borín). This dual naming reflects the historical and geographical perspectives of the local communities.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this route overlap with sections of the 'Liegos-Acevedo' trail, offering a glimpse into other local paths within the region.

waypoints.name: Horse and Cattle Mural on PR-32, Riaño Valley
